Card.title: Card
Card.description: Represents a card in the card game.

ability.description: The ability or effect of the card.

flavorText.description: Descriptive flavor text for the card.

attackPower.description: The attack power of the card.

defense.description: The defense value of the card.

manaCost.description: The mana cost to play the card.

cardType.description: The type of card (e.g., creature, spell, equipment).

cardRarity.description: The rarity of the card (e.g., common, uncommon, rare).

imagePlaceholder.description: File path or URI to the card's image placeholder.

VisualTheme.title: VisualTheme
VisualTheme.description: Represents a visual theme that can be applied to cards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the visual theme.

name.description: The name of the visual theme (e.g., fantasy, sci-fi).

description.description: A description of the visual theme.

primaryColor.description: The primary color associated with the theme (e.g., #3F51B5).

backgroundColor.description: The background color associated with the theme (e.g., #F0F0F0).

accentColor.description: The accent color associated with the theme (e.g., #FFC107).

fontFamily.description: The font family used for the theme (e.g., 'Inter').

definition.description: Stores visual theme data. Each document represents a theme that can be applied to card designs.

params.name: visualThemeId
params.description: Unique identifier for the visual theme.

definition.description: Stores card data. Includes a `visualThemeId` property to reference the visual theme applied to the card.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to manage card and visual theme data for the CardCrafter application. It emphasizes authorization independence by storing all necessary authorization information within the documents themselves, avoiding the need for `get()` calls in security rules. The structure follows the principle of structural segregation, ensuring that each collection serves a distinct purpose and has a homogeneous security posture. This approach simplifies security rules and enhances debuggability.

The core entities, `VisualTheme` and `Card`, are stored in separate collections. `VisualTheme` is stored at the root level in `/visual_themes/{visualThemeId}`. `Card` documents are stored in the `cards` collection at the root level: `/cards/{cardId}`. The `Card` document contains the `visualThemeId` to reference the `VisualTheme` document it uses. The design supports listing operations (`QAPs`) securely because all documents within each collection share the same security requirements.
